subject: Statistics
title: The Future of Polling
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Future of Polling usable.

hook.text: Response rates for phone polls have dropped from 36% in 1997 to under 6% today. Polling is in crisis.

buildup.text: People don't answer unknown calls. Caller ID, spam filters, and survey fatigue are killing phone polls.

discovery.text: Researchers are turning to voter files, social media, and probabilistic panels to find respondents.

twist.text: AI models can now predict survey responses from demographic data. But predictions aren't measurements.

climax.text: Polling is evolving, not dying. The methods will change dramatically, but the need for data won't.

punchline.text: The question isn't whether to poll. It's how.

quiz.question: Why are phone polls becoming less effective?
options: Response rates have plummeted — people don't answer unknown calls anymore
Phone technology has become too expensive
Polls are no longer useful for elections
